raw_consumer_statement:
on XX/XX/25 I noticed a charge from XXXX XXXX for {$150.00}. 
I did not make this charge. I am not a XXXX XXXX customer. 
I reported the unauthorized charge right away on XX/XX/25 within 2 minutes of the charge notification. 
On XX/XX/25 I received a notification from the Citi mobile app to call Citi Bank right away to discuss suspicious activity my account. 
I called them on XX/XX/25 at XXXX and they told me my credit card had been compromised and added to several mobile wallets that I was unfamiliar with. They told me they would cancel the card and ship a new one. During this call they asked if I had attempted to make purchases with XXXX XXXX and another wireless carrier, both of which I had not. They confirmed these were fraudulent charges and I would not be responsible. 
I received the new credit card 12 days later. I activated the new card and have not used it since. 
On XX/XX/25 I received an update on the Citi mobile app stating that the case was closed and that I was found responsible for the {$150.00} XXXX XXXX charge. 
I called Citi Bank at XXXX on XX/XX/25 and they told me that the agent I spoke to on the phone on XX/XX/25 had incorrectly filed my case as a " chargeback '' rather than a fraud case, and once a chargeback case had been " resolved '' on their end, there was nothing else they could do. They said they would try to send an email to their fraud department to re-open my case, but that it was " up to them '' and I would still be responsible for the {$150.00} if they decided not to re open the case.
